title: Meningitis: CSF analysis
body_html:
Normal CSF values
ParameterNormal RangeNotes
Opening pressure10–20 cmH₂OWith patient in lateral decubitus position
AppearanceClearTurbid or cloudy may indicate infection or inflammation
White cell count0–5 cells/mm³Lymphocytes; >5 suggests infection or inflammation
Red cell count0 cells/mm³Presence may indicate traumatic tap or subarachnoid haemorrhage
Protein0.15–0.45 g/LHigher in neonates; raised in infection, inflammation, or malignancy
Glucose2.5–4.4 mmol/L or 60–80% of plasma levelLow in bacterial, TB, and fungal meningitis
CSF:Serum glucose ratio>0.6<0.4 suggests bacterial or TB meningitis
Lactate<2.0 mmol/LElevated in bacterial meningitis

Normal CSF values compared to meningitis of various aetiology
ParameterNormalBacterial MeningitisViral MeningitisTB MeningitisFungal Meningitis
Opening Pressure10–20 cmH₂O>20 cmH₂ONormal or mildly raised>20 cmH₂OElevated or normal
AppearanceClearTurbid (cloudy)Clear or slightly cloudyCobweb clot or slightly cloudyClear or slightly cloudy
WCC0–5 cells/mm³>1,000 cells/mm³ (predominantly neutrophils)10–1,000 cells/mm³ (lymphocytes)50–500 cells/mm³ (lymphocytes)20–500 cells/mm³ (lymphocytes)
Protein0.15–0.45 g/L>1.0 g/LSlightly raised (0.5–1.0 g/L)>1.0 g/L>0.5 g/L
Glucose2.5–4.4 mmol/L or >60% of plasma<2.2 mmol/L or <40% of plasmaNormal or mildly reduced<2.2 mmol/L<2.2 mmol/L
CSF:Serum Glucose Ratio>0.6<0.4Normal or slightly reduced<0.4<0.4
Gram Stain/ZN stainNegativeUsually positive (Gram +ve/-ve organisms)NegativeAcid-fast bacilli may be seen (ZN stain)May show yeasts (e.g. Cryptococcus)
Lactate<2.0 mmol/L>3.5 mmol/LNormal or slightly raisedRaisedRaised

CSF notes
The Ziehl-Neelsen stain is only 20% sensitive in the detection of tuberculous meningitis and therefore PCR is sometimes used (sensitivity = 75%)

A low glucose may be seen in some viral causes of meningitis such as herpes encephalitis mumps
